question: In-progress autosave sanity/opportunity review for refreshed-center r03 det=1.5 MHz long-span Ramsey/T2star/13C follow-up.

protocol_basis.axis_contract_check: ExperimentDataEachAvg axis verified by averaging per-average readout axis back to ExperimentData.

summary_stats.readout_roles: ramsey.xml full_experiment=0: readout1 reference, readout2 Ramsey signal; interpretation still requires terminal review.

drift.reference_method: single-average raw readout common-mode; no cross-average reference
drift.trace_aggregation_method: mean normalized raw brightness across selected readout traces
drift.drift_score_method: max(0, -linear end-minus-start of per-average common-mode raw brightness in acquisition order when scan-order metadata exists)
drift.data_order_for_score: acquisition order if ScanOrderEachAvg is available; stored tau order otherwise

interpretation.nonterminal_caveat: This is an in-progress 12-average autosave, not terminal evidence. Do not claim T2star or 13C from it.
interpretation.opportunity_read: Autosave review has 12 saved averages (600000 shots/tau). Combined ratio LS screen is currently highest near 1.513 MHz; programmed carrier amplitude is 0.01741, expected sideband amplitudes are 0.00187/0.01299, and prior short-tau artifact-control amplitude at 1.192 MHz is 0.00198. This is progress context only; terminal data and per-average/SEM consistency remain required.
interpretation.decision: Continue the running bridge job to terminal unless a hard anomaly appears. Terminal raw export plus scan-order-aware drift review remains required before any T2star/13C conclusion.
